column10:

column28:
All users external/ internal reporting receiving an error when logging into Appstream:
Certificate-based authentication not available

First report around 1:30 Central.
Initially re-entering credentials would bypass this.
Around 3pm Central users reported not being able to get in with the workaround. 

Chen reportedly working with AWS
column571: Required for P1/P2 issues, full description of the current status.
column752: Please provide overall impact and impact by client if needed.
column934: The fields on this tab are designed for Serviceability data collection by team SE&OQ. More details please refer to https://wiki.freewheel.tv/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=450573051
column1119: 13/Oct/25 16:45;kgugerty;[~cphuah] is currently engaged with AWS AppStream Support actively troubleshooting the issue.;;;
column1120: 13/Oct/25 17:04;ahoskins;Workaround solution has been provided to customers while Chen continues to work with AWS on full resolution. At the current time only 10 users are being effected.;;;
column1121:
13/Oct/25 17:11;astefanov;Update from [~cphuah]:
- Issue is on AWS side with certificate threshold we weren't aware was in place
- Working with AWS personnel to increase threshold and resolve the issue

Meanwhile:
- Regular (non-SSO) users: they currently login using Active Directory (AD) password. The AWS issue results in a secondary login screen where they can retype their AD credentials and proceed.
- SSO users: we'll need to generate a new AD password and send it to them so they can use the workaround above.;;;
column1122:
13/Oct/25 17:46;astefanov;[~cphuah] provided update: "_It is fixed now - Please test it. Thx!_"

Multiple internal users are able to login with trainer and test accounts without the secondary prompt.;;;
column1123: 14/Oct/25 01:38;jmurphy;[~cphuah] [~astefanov] Please add more detail to what the "certificate threshold" is and pertains to.  Also - was there a reason why this threshold was just exceeded or did something change that contributed to exceeding this?;;;
column1125:
14/Oct/25 12:47;kgugerty;[~astefanov] [~jmurphy] 

AWS has an account lifetime cap of 1 million certificate requests.  This is for the entire 248790300697 [aws-strata-prd@freewheel.com|mailto:aws-strata-prd@freewheel.com] account, not just the App Stream Service.  When an account reaches that limit the certificates are denied which resulted in the error messages the clients were receiving. 

The cap can be raised upon request ( now set to 2 million, which should carry us for about 18 months).  

We were previously unaware of this cap.  

There is a case escalated with AWS for a query / report so we can monitor this threshold and receive notifications when we are again approaching the limit.

cc [~cphuah]  please correct if I misunderstood any of the above info.;;;
column1126:
17/Oct/25 11:41;astefanov;The following link was provided in a Teams meeting: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/acm/latest/userguide/acm-limits.html. It specifically lists (last row in table "The following general service quotas apply to your AWS account when using ACM"):
Number of Private Certificates per CA (lifetime) 	1,000,000;;;
column1127:
26/Oct/25 14:56;scriptrunner;This ticket has been pending for more than 5 days without updates. 
[~rchambers] please update it and reopen to ENG/OPS if you need further actions. Otherwise it will be automatically closed in 2 days.;;;
column1128: 28/Oct/25 18:02;astefanov;Per today's RCA call, the limit was actually increased to 5,000,000.;;;

column1121: 18/Apr/25 10:54;tstrege;[~vfava] [~kyasa] Both Anson (4806) and The St. Gregory Group 3389 have reported the same problem now. Samer server SQLMBAGSBMSPR23;;;
column1122:
18/Apr/25 14:41;tstrege;Keith GugertyKeith Gugerty  [1:35 PM|https://freewheel.slack.com/archives/C08NWKW9QRJ/p1745001319621969]
Root cause - Windows Updates for SQL Servers fails on automated install.  When this fails the logs produced are about 700MB in size.  This was paused after the last round of windows updates, but the pause expired and the updates resumed.
there is a current open case with Microsoft to investigate this failure.
Nagios Drive alerting for the system drive has automation in place to catch it at 10 GB, then kick off automation to clean up temp files to clear the alert.SQL Server specific monitoring has been setup to alert instead of kick off the automation.Separate automation has been setup to clear out older log files in the directory where they accumulate.;;;
column1123:
18/Apr/25 14:42;tstrege;Keith Gugerty  [1:35 PM|https://freewheel.slack.com/archives/C08NWKW9QRJ/p1745001319621969]

there is a current open case with Microsoft to investigate this failure.
